description: Update circuit-json-to-lbrn from 0.0.71 to 0.0.74 in package.json to include bug fixes and dependency updates.
body:
### Motivation
- Bring `circuit-json-to-lbrn` up to the latest published release to pick up bug fixes and dependency updates.

### Description
- Update `circuit-json-to-lbrn` from `^0.0.71` to `^0.0.74` in `package.json`.
- Refresh `bun.lock` to pin the resolved `circuit-json-to-lbrn@0.0.74` entry and update the lockfile metadata.
- No runtime source code changes were made.

### Testing
- No automated test suite was executed for this dependency-only change.
- Local verification was performed by running `npm view circuit-json-to-lbrn version` and installing via `bun add -d circuit-json-to-lbrn@latest`, which completed successfully.

title: Add editable CircuitPreview examples
description: Add functionality to the CircuitPreview component allowing users to edit circuit code snippets directly in a textarea, with changes reflected in the preview and a loading state displayed during updates.
body:
## Summary
- Make `CircuitPreview` examples switch into a textarea on click or typing so users can edit any previewed snippet
- Keep edited source in component state and feed it back into the generated preview URLs
- Add a small `loading...` overlay with faded previews while images refresh

## Testing
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run build`
- Verified in the browser on a docs page that clicking the code preview enters textarea mode, edits persist, and the loading state appears while the preview updates

title: Fix Empty Copper Pour Inputs to Preserve Full Pour Geometry
description: Fixes empty copper-pour blocker inputs to preserve the full pour geometry instead of hitting an empty manifold polygon path.
body:
Fixes empty copper-pour blocker inputs so they preserve the full pour geometry instead of hitting an empty manifold polygon path.

Changes:
- Return an empty manifold cross-section safely for empty polygon inputs.
- Add a regression test covering an empty blocker list.

title: Eliminate Copper Pour Boolean Failures with Robust Manifold Geometry Engine
description: Replaces the legacy flatten-js copper pour boolean pipeline with a new manifold-3d geometry engine, improving robustness on complex boards and dense obstacle layouts.
body:
**PR Description:**
This PR replaces the legacy `flatten-js` copper pour boolean pipeline with a new `manifold-3d` geometry engine, dramatically improving robustness on complex boards and dense obstacle layouts.

### Improvements

* Migrates copper pour subtraction / offset operations to `manifold-3d`
* Prevents boolean-operation crashes on real-world designs
* Adds ring normalization, scaling, island cleanup, and hole extraction
* Removes tiny disconnected copper artifacts automatically
* Preserves valid BRep output with consistent winding directions
* Adds comprehensive geometry regression tests

### Impact

Complex boards that previously failed during copper pour generation now solve reliably, with cleaner islands, stable outputs, and far stronger geometric correctness.

title: Set oxidation cleaning layer default to false
description: Changes the default setting of the oxidation cleaning layer to false in the WorkspaceProvider to prevent unintentional enabling during workspace creation and option merges.
body:
### Motivation
- The `includeOxidationCleaningLayer` default was `true`, which enables the oxidation cleaning layer by default and is not desired for typical conversions.  
- Change the default to `false` so new workspaces and option merges do not unintentionally enable the oxidation cleaning layer.  

### Description
- Update the initial LBRN options in `WorkspaceProvider` to set `includeOxidationCleaningLayer: false` in `lib/components/workspace-context.tsx`.  
- Ensure `setLbrnOptions` also enforces `includeOxidationCleaningLayer: false` when merging incoming option partials.  
- The change is contained to `lib/components/workspace-context.tsx` and only adjusts the default/configuration values.  

### Testing
- No automated tests were run for this small configuration/default change.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69f149de147083278bb1f2c77d962726)

title: chore: bump circuit-json-to-lbrn lockfile entry to ^0.0.74
description: Updates the Bun workspace lockfile to change the circuit-json-to-lbrn dependency version from 0.0.71 to 0.0.74 and adjusts the lbrnts version accordingly, ensuring consistency in the dependency graph without altering any source code.
body:
### Motivation
- Align the Bun workspace lockfile with the updated `circuit-json-to-lbrn` dependency and ensure the `lbrnts` lock constraint matches the resolved dependency graph.

### Description
- Edited `bun.lock` to change the workspace dependency for `circuit-json-to-lbrn` from `^0.0.60` to `^0.0.71`.
- Updated the resolved package entry from `circuit-json-to-lbrn@0.0.60` to `circuit-json-to-lbrn@0.0.71` in `bun.lock`.
- Updated `lbrnts` references in `bun.lock` from `^0.0.17` to `^0.0.18` to match the updated dependency set.
- No source code changes were made; this is a lockfile-only update to keep the lock consistent with the requested package version.

### Testing
- `npm view circuit-json-to-lbrn version` failed due to registry access returning HTTP 403 in this environment.
- `bun add circuit-json-to-lbrn@latest` failed due to registry access returning HTTP 403 in this environment.
- `git status --short` executed successfully.
- `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` executed successfully and shows the `bun.lock` change.

title: Replace logged-in sign-out CTA with dashboard navigation on landing page
description: Updates the landing page auth CTA so signed-in users see a Dashboard button that links to the dashboard instead of an in-page Sign out action, aligning button behavior with navigation-only auth flows.
body:
Update the landing page auth CTA so signed-in users see a Dashboard button that links to https://tscircuit.com/dashboard instead of an in-page Sign out action. This also removes the old sign-out cl
  ick handler and session-clearing logic, leaving the button behavior aligned with navigation-only auth flows.

title: Sync landing page auth button with stored session state
description: This PR makes the landing page auth CTA reflect the users current client-side session state.
body:
This PR makes the landing page auth CTA reflect the user's current client-side session state.

  Changes:

  - Read localStorage.session_store on page load
  - Show Sign in when there is no stored session or state.session is null
  - Show Sign out when a stored session exists
  - Update both desktop and mobile nav auth links consistently
  - Clear the stored session and immediately refresh the button state on sign out
  - Re-sync the button state on focus, pageshow, and cross-tab storage updates

  Why:

  - Fixes a UX bug where the landing page always showed Sign in even for logged-in users
  - Makes the navigation match the actual client session state without requiring a refresh

title: Update LED water accelerometer image file

title: Refactor GlobalDrcForceImproveSolver solver to use targeted error iteration and adaptive fallback
description: Refactors the GlobalDrcForceImproveSolver to implement targeted error iteration and adaptive fallback mechanisms for improved error handling in DRC processes.

title: Connect overlapping same-net obstacle regions
description: Adds obstacle-contact ports when same-net obstacle polygons overlap or touch on a shared layer, allowing both serialized obstacle regions to reference the shared contact point, with test coverage for overlapping pad regions.
body: Add obstacle-contact ports when same-net obstacle polygons overlap or touch on a shared layer. This lets both serialized obstacle regions reference the shared contact point, with test coverage for overlapping pad regions.

title: Use rectdiff-style port spacing for poly hypergraphs
description: Updates poly hypergraph port generation to match rectdiff conventions: spacing now derives from trace width plus obstacle margin, endpoint margins use 34 spacing, and long shared edges decimate dense port candidates after 5 ports. Short midpoint-only ports are marked as cramped, and tests cover the new defaults, decimation behavior, and cramped metadata.
body: before: after: Updates poly hypergraph port generation to match rectdiff conventions: spacing now derives from trace width plus obstacle margin, endpoint margins use 3/4 spacing, and long shared edges decimate dense port candidates after 5 ports. Short midpoint-only ports are marked as cramped, and tests cover the new defaults, decimation behavior, and cramped metadata. before: after:

title: Fix tsci push to work without an entrypoint
description: Fixes the tsci push command to resolve the package root without requiring an entrypoint when no file is passed, while maintaining explicit file pushes using entrypoint validation.
body:
## Summary
- Make `tsci push` resolve the package root without requiring an entrypoint when no file is passed.
- Keep explicit file pushes using entrypoint validation, but avoid failing the no-arg flow when only `package.json` and project files are present.
- Update push coverage to verify the no-entrypoint case and preserve the existing package.json-missing failure.

## Testing
- Added/updated Bun tests for push with no entrypoint and for the existing default-entrypoint path.
- Ran focused `bun test` coverage for the push command, plus formatting and type-check validation.

title: Speed Up Benchmark Profile-Solver Runs with Concurrent Workers and Cl…
description: This PR dramatically improves benchmark throughput and reliability by upgrading profile-solvers to run scenarios in parallel worker processes with per-sample timeout protection.
body:
This PR dramatically improves benchmark throughput and reliability by upgrading `profile-solvers` to run scenarios in parallel worker processes with per-sample timeout protection.

### Improvements

* Adds configurable worker concurrency (`--concurrency`, auto-supported)
* Runs profiling tasks in isolated child processes
* Adds per-scenario timeout handling and automatic worker recovery
* Tracks total benchmark runtime in JSON reports
* Improves CI benchmark summaries with solved/failed counts and timing
* Passes concurrency and timeout args through GitHub Actions benchmark workflow

### Impact

Large benchmark runs complete faster, hung scenarios no longer stall CI, and profiling reports provide clearer operational visibility and more actionable performance data.

title: Add srj13 dataset integration
description: Add tsciseveibar.dataset-srj13 as a benchmark dataset source and register it as srj13, allowing --dataset 13 across benchmark, profiling, and sample-loading entry points via dataset alias normalization, and adding a Cosmos fixture for browsing example_01 through example_50 in the new dataset with regression tests.
body:
## Summary
- Add `@tsci/seveibar.dataset-srj13` as a benchmark dataset source and register it as `srj13`
- Allow `--dataset 13` across benchmark, profiling, and sample-loading entry points via dataset alias normalization
- Add a Cosmos fixture for browsing `example_01` through `example_50` in the new dataset
- Cover the new alias and sample ordering with a regression test

## Testing
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun test tests/pipeline-stage-debug-runner.test.ts`
- Local benchmark smoke run against `--dataset 13` to confirm the dataset wiring and sample loading path

### Description
- Added a raw bug report JSON at `fixtures/bug-reports/bugreport56-44d3c9/bugreport56-44d3c9.json` containing the downloaded `simple_route_json` payload.
- Added a minimal fixture component at `fixtures/bug-reports/bugreport56-44d3c9/bugreport56-44d3c9.fixture.tsx` that loads the SRJ into `AutoroutingPipelineDebugger` for interactive inspection.
- Applied repository formatting to keep code style consistent.

- Ran `biome format --write .` to format files successfully.
- No snapshot or unit tests were added per request.

title: Promote sparse coverage into shared multilayer nodes
description: Promotes sparse single-layer free space into shared multilayer coverage when usable multilayer volume is too low.
body:
## Summary

Promote sparse single-layer free space into shared multilayer coverage when usable multilayer volume is too low.

## What changed

- add `SparseMultilayerPromotionSolver` to promote overlapping free single-layer nodes into contiguous multilayer spans
- run the sparse promotion stage from `RectDiffPipeline`
- update `OuterLayerContainmentMergeSolver` to gate the special merge path on usable multilayer volume share instead of raw area
- coalesce compatible promoted nodes and trim redundant single-layer coverage already contained inside free multilayer nodes

## Why

Some autorouter fixtures stall because rectdiff emits mostly single-layer capacity nodes even when the practical routing need is concentrated in shared upper-layer corridors. That leaves too little shared coverage for downstream port-point pathing and tiny-hypergraph to route efficiently.

This change shifts the structure toward shared nodes only when the usable multilayer volume share is low, so sparse cases can be promoted without changing already-healthy layouts.

## Impact

- increases shared multilayer node coverage for sparse problem cases
- gives downstream solvers more useful shared regions instead of fragmented single-layer nodes
- keeps the behavior gated behind a measurable threshold instead of applying globally

## Validation

- `bun run format`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`

title: Consider editor fully loaded when package metadata and priority file are ready
description: Changes the logic for determining if the editor is fully loaded to consider it ready when package metadata and the priority file are available, allowing other files to load in the background.
body:
### Motivation
- Improve perceived editor interactivity by treating the editor as ready once package metadata and the priority file are available, allowing remaining files to stream in the background.

### Description
- Update `isFullyLoaded` to return `!isLoadingPackageFiles && isPriorityFileFetched` and add a clarifying comment that remaining files continue streaming in the background.
- Remove dependence on `isLoadingPackageFilesWithContent` from the `isFullyLoaded` logic and adjust the hook dependency array to `[isLoadingPackageFiles, isPriorityFileFetched]`.

### Testing
- Ran the test suite and type-checking (`tsc`) against the modified code and all checks passed.

------

### Motivation

- Bump two dependencies to their latest published versions to pick up fixes and improvements for build and export tooling.

### Description

- Updated `@tscircuit/internal-dynamic-import` from `^0.0.4` to `^0.0.5` in `package.json`.
- Updated `circuit-json-to-gerber` from `^0.0.50` to `^0.0.51` in `package.json`.
- Regenerated `bun.lock` to record the resolved package versions and checksums for the updated packages.
- Committed the updated `package.json` and `bun.lock` reflecting the dependency bumps.

### Testing

- Ran `npm view @tscircuit/internal-dynamic-import version && npm view circuit-json-to-gerber version` and confirmed published versions `0.0.5` and `0.0.51` respectively (succeeded).
- Ran `bun add -d @tscircuit/internal-dynamic-import@latest circuit-json-to-gerber@latest` to install and update the lockfile (succeeded).
- Verified `package.json` and `bun.lock` contain the new version entries and committed the changes (succeeded).

- Apply the same search behavior across the API route, component listings, and proxy search paths
- Add unit coverage for the new token grouping behavior

## Testing
- Unit tests added for search token grouping in the root app and cf-proxy
- Local typecheck and formatting passed in both workspaces

title: Add coppertext element documentation with PCB examples and props reference
description: This PR adds a dedicated docs page for the coppertext element, including usage examples and a props reference.
body:
This PR adds a dedicated docs page for the coppertext element.

  It includes:

  - An overview of what coppertext is used for
  - A basic PCB example
  - A top/bottom copper labeling example
  - A knockout text example
  - A props table based on the exported copperTextProps
  - Notes clarifying when to use coppertext vs pcbnotetext

  This is a documentation-focused PR that fills a missing API reference for an exported @tscircuit/props element.

title: Add courtyard support for component collision boundaries
description: Adds support for PCB courtyards (pcb_courtyard_rect, pcb_courtyard_polygon, pcb_courtyard_outline) as component collision boundaries. When a component has courtyard data, the packer uses it instead of pad-based bounds for overlap detection, outline construction, and obstacle clearance  preventing component bodies from overlapping even when pads dont.
body: Adds support for PCB courtyards (pcb_courtyard_rect, pcb_courtyard_polygon, pcb_courtyard_outline) as component collision boundaries. When a component has courtyard data, the packer uses it instead of pad-based bounds for overlap detection, outline construction, and obstacle clearance — preventing component bodies from overlapping even when pads don't.

title: Fix edge-crossing PCB holes in board mesh rendering
description: Fixes rendering issues caused by edgecastellated holes that extend outside the board outline, ensuring consistent 3D output by detecting boundary-crossing holes and applying a boolean mesh cut path.
body: Edge/castellated holes can be centered on the board boundary, which means their drill geometry extends partially outside the board outline. The existing board mesh triangulation path assumed all hole loops were fully contained inside the board, causing asymmetric rendering where holes could appear on one side of the board but be filled or hidden on the other. This change detects boundary- crossing holes/cutouts and uses a boolean mesh cut path for those cases, producing consistent 3D output while preserving the fast path for normal interior holes. Before After

title: Match LightBurn SVG previews for scan/fill layers
description: Updates SVG snapshot rendering so Scan  ScanCutfill layers always preview as outlines, matching LightBurns software view, while removing hatch-pattern SVG preview logic and keeping .lbrn2 generation unchanged.
body: Updates SVG snapshot rendering so `Scan` / `Scan+Cut`/`fill` layers always preview as outlines, matching LightBurn’s software view. Removes hatch-pattern SVG preview logic while keeping `.lbrn2` generation unchanged, including scan/crosshatch settings used for actual laser behavior. Tests updated to assert scan SVGs no longer emit hatch patterns or hatch fills. ### LightBurn Screenshot: ### Before: ### Now:

title: update SVGs (Match LightBurn)
description: Updates the lbrnts dependency to version 0.0.19 in package.json

title: Introduce per-index Cutting Layers visibility controls to fix incomplete LBRN preview inspection
description: Adds a preview-only Cutting Layers dropdown beside the layer selector so users can inspect every generated LightBurn layer individually without changing exported .lbrn output.
body: Adds a preview-only Cutting Layers dropdown beside the layer selector so users can inspect every generated LightBurn layer individually without changing exported `.lbrn` output. **What changed** - Introduces per-index Cutting Layers visibility controls for all generated `CutSetting` entries. - Includes both `Cut` and `Scan` layers, fixing missing entries like `C10` and `C12`. - Shows LightBurn color swatches, `Cxx` indexes, setting type, and eye/eye-off visibility icons. - Keeps visibility filtering limited to the SVG preview, so downloads remain unchanged.

title: update lbrnts / circuit-json-to-lbrn
description: Updates the versions of circuit-json-to-lbrn and lbrnts in package.json to 0.0.77 and 0.0.19 respectively.

title: document USB-C connector standard usage
description: Documents the preferred usage of the USB-C connector standard in the tscircuit framework, advising users to utilize built-in syntax instead of importing from JLCPCB.

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.5
description: Automated package update

title: Update circuit-json-to-gerber version to 0.0.51
description: Updates the version of circuit-json-to-gerber from 0.0.50 to 0.0.51 in package.json

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.4
description: Automated package update

title: Update circuit-json-to-gerber version to 0.0.50
description: Updates the version of circuit-json-to-gerber from 0.0.48 to 0.0.50 in package.json

title: add analysis for pin to edge width distance
description: Adds analysis for pin padding to edge distance in schematic placement, identifying issues when the padding exceeds allowed limits.

title: flag schematic-box-sizing too wide / folder restructure
description: Adds functionality to identify and flag schematic boxes that are too wide for pin headers and chips, along with restructuring the folder organization for analyzers.

title: Add trace-to-pad clearance support (nudge the traces away from the pad)
description: Add minTraceToPadEdgeClearance handling to the Global DRC force-improvement solver, using the SRJ-provided clearance in obstacle repulsion and default DRC snapshot options while keeping the existing preferred clearance as fallback.
body:
## Summary
- Add `minTraceToPadEdgeClearance` handling to the Global DRC force-improvement solver
- Use the SRJ-provided clearance in obstacle repulsion, broad spatial interaction distance, and default DRC snapshot options
- Keep the existing preferred clearance as the fallback

## Testing
- Unit tests passed via the existing `bun test` suite

title: clean the codebase
description: This pull request focuses on cleaning up the codebase by removing unnecessary imports, types, and constants, thereby improving readability and maintainability.

title: Refactor DRC solver to use targeted error iteration and adaptive fall back
description: Refactors the DRC solver to implement targeted error iteration and an adaptive fallback mechanism for improved error handling and performance.

title: Refactor SRJ dataset generation to use core converter and primitive obstacle modeling
